details.publicNotes:
Conversion notes (this module)

Role: the self-styled King of Spiders — a poisoner-rogue and blackmailer, not a forced boss fight. The PCs only need the ambassador's letters; the intended path is social (Diplomacy + bribes / a game of knivesies), and the Guard won't prosecute if he is killed.

Tactics: opens by springing the throne trap door, then wades in with poisoned gauntlets and Sneak Attacks; his enforcers don't respond until he raises the alarm or a thug flees. Morale: below ~10 HP he calls for help, drinks his potion of invisibility, and flees to C14.

His secret: he can't truly command spiders — the ettercap @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.chittersnap00001]{Chittersnap} in C15 does, in exchange for victims. Kill Chittersnap and every spider on Eel's End turns feral, Devargo included.

description.value: Sometimes referred to as an alley bow by rogues or ruffians, this small crossbow fires small bolts that are sometimes used to deliver poison to the target. It's small enough to be shot one-handed, but it still requires two hands to load.
Shorter than traditional arrows but similar in construction, bolts are the ammunition used by crossbows.
A mix of flexible and molded boiled leather, a suit of this type of armor provides some protection with maximum flexibility.
Providing the same defensive function as a standard gauntlet, this version has a group of spikes protruding from the knuckles to deliver piercing damage with a punch.
An invisibility potion is colorless and oddly lightweight. Upon drinking it, you gain the effects of a 2nd-level invisibility spell.

A healing potion is a vial of a ruby-red liquid that imparts a tingling sensation as the drinker's wounds heal rapidly. When you drink a healing potion, you regain [[/r {2d8+5}[healing]]]{2d8+5 Hit Points}.

Devargo isn't flat‑footed to creatures of 5th level or lower that are @Compendium[pf2e.conditionitems.Hidden]{Hidden}, @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.VRSef5y1LmL2Hkjf]{Undetected}, flanking, or using surprise attack.

Trigger A creature within Devargo's melee reach is hit by a melee attack from one of his allies

Effect Devargo makes a Strike against the triggering creature with a -2 penalty to the attack roll. He can't deal sneak attack damage on this Strike. After this Strike, Devargo can Stride without triggering reactions.

Saving Throw @Check[type:fortitude|dc:17]

Maximum Duration 6 rounds

Stage 1 1d6 poison and @Compendium[pf2e.conditionitems.Flat-Footed]{Flat-Footed} (1 round)

Stage 2 [[/r 1d8 #poison]]{1d8 poison}, @Compendium[pf2e.conditionitems.Clumsy]{Clumsy 1}, and @Compendium[pf2e.conditionitems.Flat-Footed]{Flat-Footed} (1 round)

Stage 3 [[/r 1d10 #poison]]{1d10 poison}, @Compendium[pf2e.conditionitems.Clumsy]{Clumsy 2}, and @Compendium[pf2e.conditionitems.Flat-Footed]{Flat-Footed} (1 round).

Devargo deals an extra [[/r {1d6}[precision]]]{1d6 precision damage} to flat‑footed creatures.

@Localize[PF2E.NPC.Abilities.Glossary.SneakAttack]
On the first round of combat, creatures that haven't acted yet are @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.AJh5ex99aV6VTggg]{Flat-Footed} to Devargo.
The nature of this object is unclear.
Devargo's spider-motif studded leather, etched with a +1 armor potency rune.

publication.title: Pathfinder: Curse of the Crimson Throne (PF2e conversion, private)

description.value: Drink this potion (a single action) to regain @Damage[1d8[vitality,healing]] Hit Points.

4 doses, milked from Chittersnap's brood. Poison (injury): a creature hit by a coated weapon attempts a @Check[type:fortitude|dc:24]; on a failure it is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.i3OJZU2nk64Df3xm]{Clumsy 2} and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.MIRkyAjyBeXivMa7]{Enfeebled 1} for 1 round (Clumsy 2 and Enfeebled 2 for 4 rounds on a critical failure). Devargo's Prepare Spider Venom assumes both gauntlets are coated.

publication.title: Pathfinder: Curse of the Crimson Throne (PF2e conversion, private)

description.value: An iron ring with two keys — the shiver brewery (C9) and the sea chest in his quarters (C14) — plus a belt purse of 79 gp.

publication.title: Pathfinder: Curse of the Crimson Throne (PF2e conversion, private)
